question: What process applies to an SRO regulation that deviates from the FINMA rules on video and online identification?
Provided the criteria set out by FINMA are met, does video identification of an individual carry the same validity as identifying that person in a face-to-face meeting?
section: III. Video identification / A. Video identification on a par with in-person identification for individuals

question: Which part of an identification document must a financial intermediary's technical equipment be able to read and decrypt during video identification?
A 40-year-old mother uses video identification to open a banking relationship for her 12-year-old son. Is she herself treated as the contracting party for identification purposes?
May a financial intermediary use technical aids to compensate for poor lighting when taking the photographs required in a video identification?
A fintech lets its video identification staff improvise each interview, without any defined process or dialogue guide. Is this acceptable?

A bank designs its online onboarding so that clients submit the details required under Articles 44 and 60 AMLO-FINMA only after the video identification interview has taken place. Is this design compliant?

During a video identification interview an employee photographs the client and the identity document's data page, but skips the document's other relevant pages. Does this satisfy the photograph requirements?
Must the audio recording of the entire video identification process be filed and archived together with the photographs taken?
Can a partnership be onboarded by way of video identification, provided the applicable requirements are met?
section: B. Video identification of legal entities or partnerships

question: Photographs of an identity document were taken during an online identification that ultimately failed to meet all of the prescribed criteria. Do the photographs still count as a simple copy of an identification document?
section: IV. Online identification / A. Documents equivalent to a simple copy of an identification document for

question: If generated through one of the recognised online identification processes, is an electronic copy of an identification document equivalent to an authenticated copy?
section: B. Online identification by electronic copy of an identification document

question: During an online identification the financial intermediary detects evidence of increased risk but continues the process. Whose approval is required before the client may be onboarded?
section: B. Online identification by electronic copy of an identification document

question: In an online identification, may a document other than an official identification document issued by the respective country be accepted as proof of identity?
section: B. Online identification by electronic copy of an identification document

question: Apart from the photographs of the identity document and of the contracting party, which item must be filed and archived in an online identification, where appropriate?
section: B. Online identification by electronic copy of an identification document

question: For the bank transfer required in the online identification process, in which two countries may the contracting party's bank account be located without any further conditions?
section: B. Online identification by electronic copy of an identification document

question: Providers of the qualified electronic signatures used for online identification must be recognised in Switzerland under which federal act?
section: B. Online identification by electronic copy of an identification document

question: Besides the electronic copy of the identification document itself, what must an authentication provider attach or add to the file it provides to the financial intermediary?
gold: confirmation that the contents match the identification document
accept: confirmation that it matches the original

question: An authentication provider verifiably confirms an identity document's authenticity at the same address the client gave as their place of residence during onboarding. Does this authentication also count as a confirmation of domicile?
When a legal entity goes through online identification, must the financial intermediary request and verify photographs of the individuals carrying out the onboarding on the entity's behalf?

A company is onboarded via online identification and the required verification payment arrives from the managing director's private account rather than from an account in the company's name. Is the payment requirement met?
In the online identification of a partnership, must the financial intermediary obtain the document specifying the power of attorney?
May the declaration regarding the beneficial owner be obtained through an electronic form signed with a qualified electronic signature?
section: A. Qualified electronic signature on online declaration form

question: When a printed and hand-signed beneficial ownership form is transmitted electronically, against which document must the financial intermediary compare the signature on the form?
section: C. Electronic transmission of the signed declaration form

question: When a financial intermediary entrusts the fulfilment of AML due diligence requirements to a third party under Article 28 para. 2 AMLO-FINMA, is a written agreement required in principle?
